summary.intent: Address a second round of Codex review findings on PR #694 (ars-ui), fixing six unresolved threads across RatingGroup and TagGroup ARIA/accessibility semantics, controlled state, and form-required handling.
summary.outcome: All six review threads were patched, local unit and snapshot tests passed, full coverage gate cleared (ars-components: 98.5% line / 90.1% branch vs 90% threshold), commit 83a47a0c pushed, threads resolved, and a new @codex review triggered.

repo: The repo uses `cargo xtask ci coverage` as the authoritative coverage gate, which runs both native and wasm (Leptos + Dioxus/web) target suites and reports a combined threshold table — it is slow (multiple compile+test passes).
The ars-components crate enforces a 90% branch coverage threshold; lowering it is not acceptable — new components must ship with sufficient tests to keep the bar.

Codex review is triggered via `@codex review` PR comment and its in-progress state is visible as an `eyes` reaction on the issue; unresolved threads are queryable via GitHub GraphQL reviewThreads API.
Snapshot tests use `cargo insta accept` to update; snapshot files live under `crates/ars-components/src/data_display/<component>/snapshots/`.
The project enforces a `snapshot-reviewed` PR label workflow for tracking reviewed snapshot changes.

code.finding: RatingGroup control must derive aria-labelledby from the component label rather than an optional adapter label that may not render; aria-required belongs on the visible control, not solely on the hidden input.
Hidden input carries form metadata; required validation signal should live on the visible control element, not be duplicated on the hidden input.
TagGroup must preserve controlled selection state when transitioning away from controlled mode instead of snapping back to the stale controlled value.
TagGroup list aria-labelledby must not unconditionally point at an optional label element that may not render.
TagGroup must expose multiselect semantics (aria-multiselectable) when selection_mode allows multiple selection.
Explicitly focusing a disabled tag in TagGroup should be a no-op; prior code allowed focus to land on disabled items.

workflow: Reply to review threads with the fixing commit SHA in the body before resolving them via GraphQL mutation — this creates a clear audit trail linking fix to finding.
Poll Codex review progress via both reactions API (eyes = in progress) and GraphQL reviewThreads filtered to unresolved+codex-authored; neither alone is sufficient.
Run focused module tests (`cargo test -p ars-components --lib data_display::<module>::tests`) before the slow full coverage gate to catch regressions cheaply.
Snapshot diffs in `git diff` are the cheapest way to verify that ARIA attribute changes landed exactly as intended before pushing.

friction: Codex review took significantly longer than usual for a small PR — multiple 120-second polling intervals elapsed with no new threads, requiring sustained wait-loop maintenance.
The full `cargo xtask ci coverage` gate is very slow (native + multiple wasm targets), making the pre-push coverage confirmation expensive in wall-clock time.

TagGroup test failures required multiple iterative read/patch/test cycles due to cascading issues (multiselect semantics, disabled-focus no-op, list label) discovered only after earlier fixes compiled.
open_items: Codex may post additional findings after the re-triggered review pass completes — that loop was still open at the end of the transcript.
The wasm (Dioxus/web) coverage suite was still compiling at the last status update; final branch percentages for wasm-only paths were not confirmed separately.

No explicit confirmation that all GitHub CI checks (unit tests job) completed green after the second push — only formatting, snapshot, clippy, workspace check, and mutual exclusion were confirmed green.
